repo_notes.content:
This is virtual-ms-java, the Virtual component of the TCC LVC Smart Cities simulation platform. It is a Spring Boot 4 microservice (Java 25) that manages geofenced virtual areas and serves as the Digital Twin representation of the Mackenzie Higienópolis campus and surrounding urban environment. It represents the 'V' in LVC — real people operating simulated systems, providing the spatial awareness layer for the platform.

Stack: Java 25 (language, with sealed classes, records, pattern matching, Virtual Threads), Spring Boot 4 (application framework), Hexagonal Architecture / Ports & Adapters (strict domain isolation — zero framework imports in domain layer), JTS (Java Topology Suite — geometry computations), gRPC (client for Event Hub publishing + server for area queries), MariaDB (entity state persistence, CQRS-Write), InfluxDB (time-series projections, CQRS-Read), Vaadin 25 (reactive web UI), Flyway (schema migrations), Docker (containerization).

Three domain pillars: (1) Area Management — three area shapes (POLYGON via JTS, CIRCLE via haversine radius, CORRIDOR via minimum distance to polyline segment), seeded with five predefined areas (Mackenzie Campus, Rua da Consolação, Consolação Neighborhood, Caio Prado Bus Stop, Santos Port); (2) EntityState — write-side source of truth tracking which simulation entities (DRONE, BUS, VESSEL) are inside which areas, stored in MariaDB with composite PK (entity_id + entity_type + area_id); (3) Event Dispatching — area transition events (Checkin/Checkout) pushed to the Event Hub via gRPC and projected into InfluxDB for Grafana dashboards.

The service exposes a REST API (port 8082), a gRPC AreaService server (port 50052), and a Vaadin 25 UI. It consumes proto-shared for gRPC stub generation and relies on infra-local-shared for MariaDB, InfluxDB, and Vault services.

Key architectural patterns: Hexagonal Architecture (ports as plain Java interfaces, adapters in infrastructure), CQRS (segregated write path to MariaDB eventhub + read path from MariaDB eventhub_read and InfluxDB projections), Geofencing (real-time spatial containment evaluation with JTS and haversine), Event-Driven Integration (gRPC publish/subscribe to Event Hub), Reactive UI (Vaadin 25 with push-based updates).
repo_notes.author: Rafael Pereira

pages.title: Architecture Overview
pages.purpose: High-level overview of the Virtual component's role as the Digital Twin layer in the LVC platform. Explain the Hexagonal Architecture structure (domain/application/infrastructure layers with strict port/adapter boundaries), the CQRS segregation (write path to MariaDB + InfluxDB events, read path from MariaDB eventhub_read + InfluxDB projections), and how this service fits into the broader LVC platform alongside Event Hub, live-ms-java, and constructive-ms-python. Cover the technology stack and the three domain pillars (Area Management, EntityState, Event Dispatching).

pages.title: Domain Layer & Geofencing Logic
pages.purpose: Document the pure-Java domain model with zero framework imports. Cover: Area sealed hierarchy (PolygonArea, CircleArea, CorridorArea) and AreaPort; PointOfInterest and PointOfInterestPort; EntityState and EntityStatePort; AreaTransitionEvent sealed hierarchy (Checkin, Checkout); EventDispatchPort, SimulationEventPort, InfluxProjectionPort; TransitionRecord, LvcOrigin enum; DomainException sealed hierarchy. Deep-dive into GeofenceCalculator: POLYGON containment via JTS, CIRCLE via haversine distance, CORRIDOR via minimum perpendicular distance to polyline segments. Include the five seeded areas with their OSM identifiers and real-world coordinates.

pages.title: Application Services
pages.purpose: Document the three application services that orchestrate domain operations. AreaManagementService: CRUD for areas and POIs, delegates to AreaPort. GeofenceMonitoringService: core geofence evaluation loop — receives entity positions, computes EntityState diff against current state, fires Checkin/Checkout transition events via EventDispatchPort. EventSimulationService: Vaadin-facing manual simulation trigger for testing. Cover the ApplicationException sealed hierarchy and how services maintain hexagonal boundaries (no infrastructure dependencies).

pages.title: Persistence Layer
pages.purpose: Document the MariaDB and InfluxDB adapters implementing CQRS segregation. MariaDB Write: AreaEntity, PoiEntity (JPA with @JdbcTypeCode JSON for coordinate arrays), EntityStateMariadbAdapter (JdbcTemplate upsert with ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE on composite PK). MariaDB Read: eventhub_read database with materialized views (area_stats, event_log) consumed by Grafana. InfluxDB: InfluxDbProjectionAdapter writing area_transitions measurements (tags: area_id/entity_type/lvc/transition, fields: entity_id/area_name/lat/lon), Flux queries for history. Cover the Flyway migration list and the dual-database user isolation pattern.

pages.title: gRPC Integration
pages.purpose: Document both gRPC roles. Client side: EventHubGrpcAdapter implements EventDispatchPort and SimulationEventPort to publish AreaTransitionEvents to the Event Hub; EventHubSubscriberAdapter starts one virtual thread per entity type at startup (InitializingBean) with persistent subscription stream and auto-reconnect on disconnect. Server side: AreaGrpcService implements the AreaServiceGrpc stub on port 50052, used by constructive-ms-python to query drone area membership. Cover EventHubGrpcConfiguration (ManagedChannel, stub beans) and EventHubProperties.

pages.title: REST API
pages.purpose: Document all REST endpoints exposed by AreaController, PoiController, and SimulationController. Cover: endpoint paths, HTTP methods, request/response DTOs, OpenAPI interface contracts (api/ package), HTTP status codes, validation, and the GlobalExceptionHandler mapping. Include Swagger UI access at /swagger-ui.html and the JacksonConfiguration for JSON serialization of geometry types.

pages.title: Vaadin 25 UI
pages.purpose: Document the reactive Vaadin 25 web interface. Cover: MainLayout (navigation shell), AreaManagementView (create/edit/delete areas with shape-specific coordinate forms — polygon vertices, circle center+radius, corridor polyline), PoiManagementView (POI CRUD), SimulationView (manual entity position submission via EventSimulationService for testing geofence transitions), MonitoringView (live area membership display with push-based updates). Explain how the UI ties into the application layer without bypassing hexagonal boundaries.

pages.title: Database Schema & Migrations
pages.purpose: Document the MariaDB virtual_areas database schema via Flyway migration scripts in src/main/resources/db/migration/. List each migration file, the tables it creates/alters (areas, pois, entity_states), column types, indexes, constraints, and the seeded data (five predefined areas with their geometries). Cover the InfluxDB bucket name (projections), measurement schema (area_transitions), and how the schema relates to infra-local-shared's init scripts.

pages.title: Configuration & Deployment
pages.purpose: Document application.properties / application.yml configuration: DataSource (MariaDB virtual_areas database, user virtual_areas), InfluxDB connection (InfluxDbProperties), EventHub gRPC target (EventHubProperties — host localhost, port 50051), Vaadin settings, server port 8082, gRPC server port 50052. Cover the two-step build process (proto-shared mvn install → virtual-ms-java mvn package), the Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml integration with infra-local-shared's shared-infra-network, and the startup dependency chain.
